management_notes:
G2 Goldfields was founded by Patrick Sheridan, an experienced exploration geologist and Guyana specialist who led multiple gold discoveries in the country. The technical team's ability to consistently hit high-grade gold at Oko — with the Oko Main Zone (OMZ) grading 7.98 g/t Au Indicated and 4.60 g/t Inferred — speaks to geological targeting capability. The company advanced from early exploration to a maiden PEA in December 2025, a meaningful de-risking milestone for a Guyana-focused junior.

Guyana is a jurisdiction where management relationships with the government and the Guyana Geology and Mines Commission (GGMC) are critical. G2 has maintained its concessions and progressed through the exploration-to-study stage without reported permitting issues. The project was initially an Indicated-heavy discovery that has now been supported by a PEA — a logical progression. Management issued a Corporate and Exploration Update in October 2025 and has ramped drilling to 6 rigs by December 2025.

A detailed insider ownership profile (SEDI transactions, proxy disclosures) was not confirmed in public data reviewed for this research cycle, which limits confidence in Factor 1. The management team's demonstrated technical execution earns the base score, but the lack of confirmed insider ownership data and the geographic/jurisdictional complexity of Guyana operations constrains the score to 3/5.
geology_notes:
G2 Goldfields published an updated Mineral Resource Estimate (MRE) in November 2025 across all discoveries: 1.6 Moz Au at 3.24 g/t Au (Indicated) and 1.9 Moz Au at 3.31 g/t Au (Inferred). The Oko Main Zone (OMZ) alone contains 808,000 oz Au Indicated at 7.98 g/t — exceptionally high grade for a resource of this scale — within 3.15 Mt. The Ghanie Gold Zone adds bulk-tonnage lower-grade material (2.64 g/t Au Inferred) amenable to open-pit mining. Total project resource is 3.5 Moz with a good distribution between high-grade underground (OMZ) and bulk-tonnage open pit (Ghanie).

The December 2025 maiden PEA outlines a 14-year mine life generating 281,000 oz Au/year (years 2-11 average) at an AISC of USD$1,137/oz, with after-tax NPV5% of USD$2.6 billion and IRR of 39% at USD$3,000/oz gold. The PEA is at the lowest economic study tier — confidence range ±35-50% — and the majority of the economic base (Ghanie zone) relies on Inferred resources that cannot be used for reserve estimation. At least 50% of the current resource is Inferred.

No reserve conversion yet. No Pre-Feasibility Study. The MRE was prepared by Micon International Limited (effective date March 1, 2025, with the Nov 2025 update). Score: 3/5 — strong grade and scale in the Indicated category, but study tier is PEA-only with significant Inferred reliance. An updated NI 43-101 technical report was filed on SEDAR+ following the December 2025 PEA.
capital_notes:
G2 Goldfields had approximately 258,464,020 shares outstanding as of the December 2025 PEA announcement period. Market capitalisation at CA$5.80/share is approximately CA$1.50 billion. The company has a TSX listing (graduated from TSXV) which provides access to institutional capital.

Specific cash and working capital figures for the most recent quarter were not confirmed in the data reviewed for this research. The PEA requires initial capital of USD$664 million (including 20% contingency), which is a substantial construction cost for a junior mining company with no operating revenue. Guyana is a country where project financing can face challenges due to lender familiarity and in-country risk premiums. The sustaining capital requirement over the LOM is an additional USD$366 million. To fund this, G2 will require either a major mining company partner, a project-level debt facility, or an equity raise of significant magnitude — each of which carries dilution or partnership risk.

The company's strength on this factor is its TSX listing and the credibility of the PEA economics at current gold prices. The weakness is the absence of operating cash flow and the large construction capital requirement relative to the company's current capitalisation. Score: 3/5.
catalyst_notes:
G2 Goldfields has a well-defined catalyst pathway following the December 2025 PEA. The logical next step is a Pre-Feasibility Study (PFS), which would upgrade at least a portion of the Inferred resource to Indicated and convert Indicated to Probable Reserves — a major de-risking event that typically drives significant re-rating for exploration-to-development juniors. With 6 drill rigs active at Oko as of Q4 2025, the company is generating ongoing news flow (drill results) that provides near-term price catalysts.

Stifel Nicolaus raised their price target on GTWO to CA$8.50 from CA$5.75 in February 2026, reflecting growing sell-side recognition of the Oko project's scale and quality. A PFS commencement announcement would likely be the next major catalyst and would bring the project into the range of interest for mid-tier and major gold producers looking for acquisition targets in a strong gold price environment. Government of Guyana interactions and permitting milestones (environmental baseline studies, EIA submission) would also be material news.

The broader Guyana gold narrative — anchored by Gold Fields' Salares Norte and ExxonMobil's offshore oil wealth transforming the country's infrastructure and governance credibility — provides a positive macro backdrop for a PFS-level de-risking. Score: 4/5.
acquisition_notes:
PEA NAV calculation for GTWO: After-tax NPV5% of USD$2.6 billion at USD$3,000/oz gold base case (December 2025 PEA). Converting to CAD at approximately 1.41 CAD/USD = CA$3.67 billion. Divided by 258.5 million shares = CA$14.19/share (unadjusted PEA NAV). Applying the mandatory 50% PEA discount (±35-50% study confidence, with Inferred resource reliance): adjusted NAV = CA$7.10/share. At CA$5.80 current price: adjusted P/NAV = 0.82x.

This falls within the 3/5 range (0.5-1.0x based on a PEA). Note that the PEA uses USD$3,000/oz gold as a base case; at current spot gold of approximately USD$4,637/oz, the real unadjusted NPV is likely 50-70% higher, making the actual discount to intrinsic value considerably deeper. However, applying the framework strictly (50% PEA discount, PEA base case gold price), the adjusted P/NAV is 0.82x.

Comparable transactions: High-grade underground gold deposits in politically stable South American jurisdictions have transacted at USD$100-200/oz Indicated + Probable resource ounces. At 1.6 Moz Indicated (counting at 60% for Indicated): 960K oz × USD$150/oz = USD$144M comparable transaction value, vs USD$1.71B market cap — suggesting the market is pricing in the Ghanie open-pit optionality and district upside. PEA-level project: 3/5.
analyst_summary:
G2 Goldfields earns a WATCH with a composite score of 16/25. The Oko West project in Guyana is a genuine discovery: 1.6 Moz Au at 3.24 g/t Indicated (with the OMZ high-grade zone averaging 7.98 g/t) and a maiden PEA outlining USD$2.6B after-tax NPV at USD$3,000/oz gold. The company is well-positioned for the next major de-risking milestone — a Pre-Feasibility Study — supported by 6 active rigs and growing sell-side coverage. Stifel Nicolaus holds a Buy with CA$8.50 target vs current CA$5.80 price.

The key risks are study-tier and jurisdictional. The project is at PEA level only — the economic confidence range is ±35-50%, and the majority of the economic base relies on Inferred resource that has not been converted to Indicated. At least one additional study (PFS) and further drilling are required before a construction decision. Guyana presents real jurisdictional complexity: GGMC permitting, community relations, and the lack of established mine-financing infrastructure in the country add execution risk. The initial capex of USD$664 million is a large sum for a company with no operating cash flow.

The catalyst to watch is a PFS commencement announcement (likely H1 2026), which would trigger a major de-risking re-rate and bring Oko firmly onto the radar of potential acquirers. At current gold prices of USD$4,637/oz vs the PEA base of USD$3,000/oz, the upside leverage to the project NAV is exceptional — a PFS completed at spot gold would show multiples of the current market cap as NPV. The WATCH rating reflects the PEA-only stage; a PFS upgrade to positive status would warrant re-evaluation toward BUY.
